title: gnaphalium pilulare
intro: Gnaphalium pilulare, also known as the pill-billy buttons, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing skin irritations. Gnaphalium pilulare can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various forms.
